extra.reasoning:
Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) jointly true.

rec_drug_a is set true only by r1 [pri 50], which fires exactly when (hyperkalemia AND seizure_history AND ckd_dialysis). No other rule sets rec_drug_a, and the default is false. So rec_drug_a = true iff H∧S∧C.

rec_drug_b is affected by two rules: r3 [pri 90] sets it false when (hyperkalemia AND seizure_history AND ckd_dialysis); r5 [pri 50] sets it true when hyperkalemia. Default false.

The only region where rec_drug_a can be true is H∧S∧C. In that exact region, both r3 (set false, priority 90) and r5 (set true, priority 50) match for rec_drug_b. Conflict resolution is by priority, so r3 wins (90 > 50) and rec_drug_b := false. Therefore, whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is necessarily false.

Hence rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b is unsatisfiable across the entire input space. The mutual-exclusion property holds. (The higher priority on r3 is precisely what protects the property; if r3 had priority ≤ 50 with ties broken by order favoring r5, this would have been violated at H∧S∧C — but as specified it holds.)
